subject: AAF default config update and fixes
commit_message:
AAF default config update and fixes

Update AAF default conf to :
- fix typo in default identity file
- add default email addresses to all pre defined users, as this was creating issues for some clients when trying to decode certs (missing Sponsor email for RFC822Name in generated certificates see JIRA : PORTAL-893).
- updated portal roles: portal does sync roles with AAF but was using another identity (aaf_admin), this has been changed in portal to use its X509 artifacts when doing REST queries to AAF, hence adding more roles to portal user to support its functions.

Issue-ID: PORTAL-875
Issue-ID: PORTAL-893
Signed-off-by: ChrisC <christophe.closset@intl.att.com>
Change-Id: I5afa2769a4ec33c1ea2f4da2f5b6e0b4938ea88f

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Christophe,
> 
> Have these changes been loaded into a working aaf database, or are they proven correct by inspection only?

John,

Here is what I did :

- Made new AAF snapshot images locally with these changes
- Spin up AAF & Portal with OOM charts locally by changing them to point to my local registry with the snapshot images
- Login to Portal and verify it worked by looking at AAF logs
- check the generated certificate for portal and verified that it included the RFC822Name (email of sponsor)

Maybe this can also be proven on one of the test lab in windriver (by overriding the aaf images to the snapshot with this fix)

Patch Set 1:

All,
  The current process is to treat the data in ONAP's AAF Test Environment as "Golden Source".  If these user_role.dat changes were not placed there, then these will be overwritten next time data is pulled from there (the current process).
Change has been successfully merged by John Franey

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1
> 
> @John Franey - would it be possible to releae a container with this change?
> This would help to test the updates within ONAP Portal, where certain testing is blocked.

Sure, but I think you mean, immediately, right?

I have these issues to work through to drive a release immediately:
1) I'm testing a code change for an AAF high priority fix; that would have to wait (still further).
2) I would like to release one image, but if I release one now, there would be another to follow to include the fix I'm working on.
3) I need a tutorial on building docker release and integrating to oom.  I have not yet done it.  I'm worried because I drove AAF's last maven release (to push to nexus) and I fouled it up.  Because I was learning, that release took two weeks start to finish.

If I could make it so with the click of my fingers, I would not even have bothered with this reply.  😊

If I could get assistance, you can get a release sooner.

John

Patch Set 1:

Well, I don`t need this immediately, as I am not completely blocked, but it would be nice to release a container after this other high-prio fix testing is completed. I`ll be looking at aaf/authz images.
(BTW. I noticed we have here a number of new fields filled in in the role configuration. 
This might be worth to test - after an image is available).
